title: Accessibility at IRCC - Canada.ca
content:
## Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Accessibility
We’re committed to providing inclusive and accessible services for people with disabilities .
- respect for the dignity of people
- equal opportunity to access and apply for our services
- the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms , Canadian Human Rights Act , and Accessible Canada Act

## Get accessible services
Tell us about your accessibility needs so we can help you better. This won’t affect the status of your application.

### How to request accessible services
Request accessible services or documents and discuss your individual accessibility needs.

### Share your thoughts and experiences
Learn more about how our feedback process works and share feedback on
- our accessibility plan
- any accessibility barriers you have experienced with Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

## Our accessibility plan and reports
Policies, programs, practices, and services that help our organization contribute to the goal of an accessible and barrier-free Canada
Results of our activities to improve how our organization contributes to the goal of an accessible and barrier-free Canada
How easily an individual can
- use a product, service or program
- access an environment
Any impairment or limitation that can prevent a person’s full and equal participation in society when they face a barrier.
Disabilities can be physical, mental, intellectual, communicative or sensory. They can be permanent, temporary or episodic.
